title: A conservative update with zero scale leaves the state unchanged

source.notes: A basic sanity theorem for the conservative update definition.

informal.statement: If the conservative update scale is zero, then the update u_i - scale * (flux(i+1)-flux(i)) equals the original state u_i.
assumptions: u is a real-valued grid state
flux is a real-valued grid flux
i is a natural-number grid index
informal.expected_conclusion: The conservative update equals the original state at index i.

declarations.name: conservativeUpdate
declarations.statement: def conservativeUpdate (scale : Real) (u flux : Nat -> Real) (i : Nat) : Real := u i - scale * fluxDifference flux i

declarations.name: conservativeUpdate_zero_scale
declarations.statement: theorem conservativeUpdate_zero_scale (u flux : Nat -> Real) (i : Nat) : conservativeUpdate 0 u flux i = u i

tasks.input: Translate the zero-scale conservative update identity into Lean 4 using conservativeUpdate.
tasks.target: theorem conservativeUpdate_zero_scale (u flux : Nat -> Real) (i : Nat) : conservativeUpdate 0 u flux i = u i

tasks.input: Given the conservativeUpdate definition and theorem statement, complete the Lean 4 proof.
tasks.target: A proof by unfolding conservativeUpdate and closing with ring.
